(1) which read as follows: “If any provision of the Metropolitan Washington Airports Amendments Act of 1996 (title IX of ; ) or the amendments made by the Act, or the application of that provision to a person, circumstance, or venue, is held invalid by a judicial order, the Secretary of Transportation and the Metropolitan Washington Airports Authority shall be subject to from the day after the day the order is issued.”Pub. children.heading: .—Separability
children.label: a .—Separability
children.text: If any provision of this chapter, or the application of a provision of this chapter to a person or circumstance, is held invalid, the remainder of this chapter and the application of the provision to other persons or circumstances is not affected.

children.heading: .—Effect of Judicial Order
children.label: b .—Effect of Judicial Order
children.text: Any action of the Airports Authority that was required to be submitted to the Board of Review under section 6007(f)(4) of the Metropolitan Washington Airports Act of 1986 (; ; ; ) before , remains in effect and may not be set aside only because of a judicial order invalidating certain functions of the Board.Public Law 99–500100 Stat. 1783–380Public Law 99–599100 Stat. 3341–383October 9, 1996
